perturbation.original_text: This Schedule ("Schedule") is made as of the effective date set forth above to that Software License, Customization, and Maintenance Agreement, by and between Cardlytics, Inc. ("Supplier") and Bank of America, N. A, ("Bank of America"), dated November 5, 2010, as amended ("SLCMA").
perturbation.changed_text: This Schedule ("Schedule") is made as of the effective date set forth above to that Software License, Customization, and Maintenance Agreement, by and between Cardlytics, Inc. ("Supplier") and Bank of America, N. A, ("Bank of America"), dated November 5, 2010, and without any amendments ("SLCMA").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'as amended' to 'without any amendments' directly contradicts the preceding clause that states the agreement is being made to a previously amended agreement, creating a conflict about the valid and enforceable terms.
perturbation.law_explanation: The UCC Section 2-209 governs the modification, rescission and waiver of contracts for the sale of goods. The modification stating 'without any amendments' contradicts the provision as this agreement Schedule document states that this agreement has been amended before. This would lead to issues of contract enforceability.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-209. Modification, Rescission and Waiver. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-209. Modification, Rescission and Waiver. (1) An agreement modifying a contract within this Article needs no consideration to be binding. (2) A signed agreement which excludes modification or rescission except by a signed writing cannot be otherwise modified or rescinded, but except as between merchants such a requirement on a form supplied by the merchant must be separately signed by the other party. (3) The requirements of the statute of frauds section of this Article (Section 2-201 ) must be satisfied if the contract as modified is within its provisions. (4)Although an attempt at modification or rescission does not satisfy the requirements of subsection (2) or (3) it can operate as a waiver. (5) A party who has made a waiver affecting an executory portion of the contract may retract the waiver by reasonable notification received by the other party that strict performance will be required of any term waived, unless the retraction would be unjust in view of a material change of position in reliance on the waiver. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modification from 'as amended' to 'without any amendments' creates a legal contradiction because it directly opposes the initial statement implying previous modifications, violating the principle that contract modifications, rescissions, and waivers are permissible under UCC Section 2-209. The original contract acknowledges amendments, while the changed version denies them, introducing inconsistency. Snippet 1, § 2-209, explicitly addresses contract modification. This change impacts contract enforceability as it creates uncertainty over the governing terms.
